profile: BENCH SAFETY — RAM-only guardrails for first serial spin
_about: Apply RAM-only (NO --save) before the first motor-in-the-loop test. Reversible by power-cycle. Caps current, tightens the command-loss watchdog, and makes SERIAL the sole command authority by removing the analog-command actions (kills the floating pin-4 hazard). Analog voltages still READ fine in ?AI/?PI diagnostics — AINA only controls whether an input ACTS as a command.
_warning: MOTOR POWER OFF while applying and during the tool hand-off. Values land in RAM only.

items.note: current limit Motor 1 = 5.0 A (0.1A units). May clamp up to 10 A (100) if the controller floors at 10 A — read-back will show. PSU current limit is the real 5 A backstop.
serial command-loss watchdog = 500 ms. Tool streams !G at ~66-116 ms, so ample margin; motor self-stops 0.5 s after serial goes silent. Tighten to 300 later if desired.
ANA3 = pin 4 (dead/floating) — remove its Motor-1 command action so a floating input can never drive the motor on serial fallback.
ANA4 = pin 17 (working analog) — remove command action so ONLY serial !G commands Motor 1 during bench testing. Re-enable later to use the analog/DMX path.
